content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 25, 1990


Mr. Mark L. Hefter
Kronick, Moskovitz, Tiedemann & Girard
770 L Street, Suite 1200
Sacramento, CA  95814-3363

                                    Re:  Your Request for Telephone Advice
                                         Our File No. T-90-582

Dear Mr. Hefter:

     On September 5, 1990, you were provided telephone advice by
this agency in response to your question whether, under provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ your client could donate
surplus campaign funds to a fund that compensates families of
police officers killed in the line of duty.  We have received your
letter of September 5, 1990, requesting written confirmation of
our telephone advice.

     Your letter correctly states our advice.  Donation of surplus
funds to charitable organizations is permitted under Section
85807(c) so long as no substantial part of the proceeds will have
a material financial effect on the former candidate, any member of
his or her immediate family, or his or her campaign treasurer.  A
fund established to aid families of deceased law enforcement
officers would appear to be the type of charitable organization
described in Section 85807(c).

     I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested.  If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                    Sincerely,

                                    Scott Hallabrin
                                    Acting General Counsel
                                    By: Peggy Bernardy
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

SH:PB:aa


_________________________
     Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: Donation of surplus funds to charitable organizations is permitted under section 85807(c) so long as no substantial part of the proceeds will have a material financial effect on the former candidate, any member of his or her immediate family, or his or her campaign treasurer. A fund established to aid families of deceased law enforcement officers would appear to be the type of charitable organization described in Section 85807(c).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate can donate surplus campaign funds to a charitable fund that compensates families of police officers killed in the line of duty under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC confirms that donating surplus campaign funds to a charitable fund for families of deceased law enforcement officers is permitted under Section 85807(c) of the Political Reform Act, provided the donation has no material financial effect on the candidate, their family, or campaign treasurer.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is written confirmation of prior telephone advice. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present, but content clearly delineates the legal question and FPPC's response. Document is letter format confirming previous oral advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a candidate can donate surplus campaign funds to a charitable fund that compensates families of police officers killed in the line of duty under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Donation of surplus funds to charitable organizations is permitted under section 85807(c) so long as no substantial part of the proceeds will have a material financial effect on the former candidate, any member of his or her immediate family, or his or her campaign treasurer. A fund established to aid families of deceased law enforcement officers would appear to be the type of charitable organization described in Section 85807(c).

A fund established to aid families of deceased law enforcement officers would appear to be the type of charitable organization described in Section 85807(c). embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephonic advice that a candidate may donate surplus campaign funds to a charitable organization that compensates families of police officers killed in the line of duty, consistent with Section 85807(c) of the Political Reform Act.
